text:
Once upon a time, in a forest deep within the mountains, there was a cottage hidden by giant ferns. Every summer, birds would gather there to listen to the old owl, Wisdom-Eye, share tales of adventures from beyond the mountain range.

It wasn’t just the stories that enchanted the birds—the wisdom, too. Wisdom-Eye didn’t just know things; he understood history and prophecy.

One twilight, a curious parrot named Perceptive wanted to know about humanity. Wisdom-Eye told him of kingdoms fallen and heroes who had soared.

"I heard there was once a great empire," Perceptive remarked thoughtfully. "A king who could command the winds?"

"Exactly!" replied Wisdom-Eye. "But he was betrayed by his own kin. He flew into the sea, never to land."

"And never found peace?"

"Never found peace. Soaring on the wind across a thousand seas?"

Perceptive pondered this. "That sounds... sad."
